ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brianna Mannion, Esq., counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator, Special Administrator (appearing by video conference); Thomas Reed, Esq., counsel for Gary Velikanje, Administrator (appearing by video conference, audio only).

At the calendar call, Mr. Thomas advises the Court of Mr. Velikanje's absence due to ill-health. At the Court's inquiry, Ms. Mannion reports on the retention of a $45,000 reserve by the Public Administrator, noting that pursuant to her inquiries in response to the Examiner Notes, she was advised that the Public Administrator has always done this when possible. After discussion, Court and counsel agree that in the future, the Public Administrator will seek prior Court approval for any such reserves.

In response to the Court's inquiry as to the deposit of funds into Mr. Reed's client trust account, Ms. Mannion notes that the check by the Public Administrator was made out to the "Estate of Patrick Edward Nash" and that the Court might best direct the question to Mr. Reed.

Mr. Reed responds, advising the Court that the estate check was deposited into his client trust account due to the unavailability of the administrator. In any event, the funds did not remain in the trust account for any significant length as they were shortly thereafter deposited to the Estate account. Further, given the paultry prevailing interest rate applicable to such accounts [reportedly 0.01%], any diminished interest payment to the estate would be de minimus.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matters under submission, indicating an intention to grant the petitions.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (D204)

Amy Harrington for Henry Castillo.
Thomas Reed for Gary Velikanje.

Ms. Harrington stated that they spoke outside of court and that they think the matter should be set for trial in about six months. The Court stated that there is a notice issue and that it can't set the matter for trial. Mr. Weinkauf stated that he thinks sufficient genealogy has been done and that he should be able to file a declaration of due diligence regarding the search for Heirs. Mr. Reed stated that his Client has also been active in locating relatives. Ms. Harrington also stated that she provided an heirship chart to Counsel, that she believes all of the people who have filed a request for special notice have received notice, but she can check on it, that depositions still need to be taken and that written discovery has been propounded and responded to. All three petitions (2-Petitions for Letters of Administration and 1-Petition for Probate of Will) are continued to June 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a determination of whether the matter can procedurally go forward. If it is to go forward, the parties are to have met and conferred with respect to the shape of the trial.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Continued To Jan-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Kyle Johnston and Amy Harrington for (petitioner) Henry Castillo. Thomas Reed for (petitioner) Gary Velikanje.

The Court stated that there are 3 petitions, that none of them has noticed all heirs who may not yet be known and asked if the parties have talked to each other or if there is interest in the Public Administrator handling this. Mr. Reed stated that they are contesting the will, that they have located 9 first cousins, that they now need to identify the deceased heirs and that they are concerned that there is no death certificate yet. Mr. Johnston stated that validating the family tree will be expensive, that the parties are reluctant to pay, that they had preliminary conversations regarding having a trial on admitting the will, they then may be able to share costs or that all parties will consent to hiring an heir finder. The Court stated that the will contest will need to be noticed on all heirs. Mr. Reed stated that they have notified the 9 cousins, that he has received the examiner's notes but was unable to talk to his client. Mr. Weinkauf stated that John Boessenecker is also involved and may represent an heir finder. Mr. Reed stated that he is trying to file an association of counsel. Ms. Harrington stated that it will be very expensive. The Court stated that maybe the heir hunter will choose to do some of the research. Mr. Weinkauf asked if he should withdraw his petition and the Court responded that it is up to him. The Court continued the two petitions for administration and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary to January 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and instructed the parties to talk to each other.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
